variation type: Nucleotide variant detected in multi-copy gene
breakpoint standard: EUCAST Legionella Guidance Document v2 (30 May, 2021)
evidence code: ECO:0006404 experimentally evolved mutant phenotypic evidence, ECO:0001113 point mutation phenotypic evidence
evidence limitations: lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: The punctual mutation A2052G in 3/3 copies of the 23S rRNA of L. pneumophila has been reported in one environmental and one clinical ST188 isolate and associated to high MICs toward C14-15 macrolides (azithromycin, erythromycin, and clarithromycin (>1024 mg/l) but moderately elevated MIC towards C16 macrolides like josamycin (= 8mg/L). The mutations A2052G/C/T have been described in experimentally evolved mutants. The resistance level is depending on the number of the mutated copies (from 1 to 3)

breakpoint standard: EUCAST Legionella Guidance Document v2 (30 May, 2021)
evidence code: ECO:0001113 point mutation phenotypic evidence
evidence limitations: low clinical relevance, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: Described in vitro in Legionella micdadei, Legionella longbeachae and Legionella pneumophila. Rifampicin is not recommended and should never be used as monotherapy in the treatment of Legionnaires' disease, limiting the risk for resistance. Numbering is according to that for the rpoB gene of L. pneumophila. Identified in Legionella longbeachae (no submitted sequence on NCBI, and a blast showed an AA similar between Legio species at position A528)
